target.name: Boom Supersonic

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Boom Supersonic is a Series D-stage frontier-tech company in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, currently valued at $1.5B post-money following $600M+ in total fundraising. The addressable peer set is constrained: the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector contains only three identified comparables—two public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and one private (Hermeus)—insufficient for robust median-based valuation.

Given the narrow peer universe, Stage-Progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation approach. This method applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D to Series E/later-stage transitions, yielding an implied valuation of $2.2B from the $1.5B last post-money round. The 1.5x multiplier reflects normalized venture progression at this capital stage.

The valuation analysis supports a range of $1.9B to $2.6B, centered on $2.2B. This range brackets the median estimate with ±15% bounds. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient same-stage comparables in the narrow Supersonic & Hypersonic sector—forcing reliance on stage mechanics rather than peer multiples—and nascent market dynamics for commercial supersonic aircraft. Further substantiation would require either expanded peer data from adjacent aerospace segments or concrete customer commitments and development milestones from Boom itself.
